dimControl 0.1.0
  • Reference

Simulated point cloud of a steel panel

Source: R/panelPoints.R
panelPoints.Rd

This dataset contains a synthetic point cloud generated from a processed theoretical CAD mesh of a steel panel.

Format

A numeric matrix with 5,000,000 rows and 3 columns:

X

X coordinate of the point

Y

Y coordinate of the point

Z

Z coordinate of the point

Source

Simulated data generated from a processed theoretical CAD model.

Details

Points are sampled over the mesh triangles with probability proportional to their area, and truncated Gaussian noise is added to simulate the uncertainty inherent to 3D scanning.

The dataset is intended for testing, comparative evaluation, and demonstration of dimensional control algorithms.

The point cloud is generated by sampling a fixed number of points over the triangles of a theoretical CAD mesh representing the surface of a steel panel. Points are assigned to each triangle with probability proportional to its area. Truncated Gaussian noise with zero mean and bounded support is added independently to each coordinate to represent scanning uncertainty.

On this page
